reviews.review_description: I used to box in the amatuer when I was younger and this is the most true to life VR boxing game we have today. I also own Thrill of the Fight 2 and it's a good boxing game also but the training isn't as realistic and the fights are a bit too demanding, but in Golden Gloves the gym and equipments are very accurate in it's physics even down to the speed bag, and finally they have mitts training, which is one of the most important things to make you a good boxer and teach you combos and movement. The fighting is also good since you don't have to exert yourself like Thrill of the fight, it's demanding but not as much as the aforementioned. Get this game if you want to train boxing

I’ve been waiting for something to top thrill of the fight 1 for fun/workout. I feel like this is it—great game, can’t believe i haven’t heard of it before. 

There is a training mode that has you throw punches/dodge which is great in its simplicity and very similar to Les Mills combat. Great workout!!
We use this for our amateur boxers , community programs, schools it's out standing highly recommend it to everybody. It's a must have you don't have to be a boxing fan to enjoy this 🥊🥊
This game is literally so bad the bot doesnt even catch ur punches literally if u fight so hard the bot wont get ko never buy get totf1 or 2 i couldnt even get an refund🤧

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear that you had a bad experience with Golden Gloves. We arent in control of refunds but you can read more about Meta's refund policy here - https://www.meta.com/help/quest/828412189021014/?sid=8655be87-22f4-4d0d-a6b3-34827dc22fe6

We are always open to feedback and would greatly appreciate it if you could give us more information about what you didn't like about the game. You can reach us in the Golden Gloves VR Discord here - https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

reviews.review_title: Good but I have some suggestions
reviews.review_description: Can you guys make the AI have better form and footwork. And can you guys add walkouts and make a better career mode. Other than that, perfect game.

reviews.review_title: Takes the cake for VR boxing
reviews.review_description: When it comes to the training side of things this games takes the lead by far, most realistic, has every all boxing bags you’d find in a real gym and the only boxing game that has pad work that is near identical to working pads in real life, only thing about the game is I prefer the first thrill of the fights sparring/ fight optics but honestly if I had to choose one that still wouldn’t be enough to win me over from this game, don’t regret my decision to purchase at all.

reviews.review_title: Mixed reality mode isn't working  proper
reviews.review_description:
The MR mode on the Quest 3 isn't working properly. I need it because of lack of space. That was also the reason for acquiring this quite good game. Cool calorie counter, awesome gym, great gameplay. But only in VR. I need plenty of space because I want to be able to jump around and not just bump into the wall. The opponents, referees, and equipment are floating 15-20 cm above the ground. My floor level is correctly calibrated in the Quest settings. Please fix this bug!

Update:
Upped to 5 stars! Even though the MR mode still has that height bug for me, the developer (Wild) actually took the time to test it and get back to me immediately. That's rare for a 14€ game. Great workout, burned 400 cal in one session. Motivation is through the roof. Cheers, Wild!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review and bug report! We will take a look at this bug today (3/5/26) and hopefully have a fix for it out soon!
- Wild

Update (3/5/26): I just tested the MR scene on a fresh build and redid my room setup. Everything seems to be working correctly on my end. I would like to discuss this with you further so I can help fix your problem. Please hop on over to our discord at https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm so we can discuss this issue more!

Thank you,
Wild

reviews.review_description: Out of all boxing games, this one is the WORST. underdog and totf2 are WAY better. Its boring, and not that much to do. I WANT A REFUND!!

I played TotF and TofF2 both have better fighting with bots. Here the bots dont react to the punches much. The pad training called fist of fury is good but not great. Hope the developers will work in it. For example the speed the combinations should change with level progress.

developer_response.body: Hi and thanks for playing. We are a small team and have had all of our focus on multiplayer and large scale tournament system for eSports.  We will continue to add features and updates, including to mitts and single player AI.  In game classes coming soon as well. If you haven't joined our Discord please do so and keep the feedback coming. Hit me up anytime there. @coachsloan

reviews.review_title: I thought hard about buying this.....
reviews.review_description: I do have Thrill of the Fight and wondered what boxing game could top that. Well, from a training perspective, this game is far better. There has been a recent update and I feel my time and money has been well spent
It feels good. Physics are comparable to totf1, which is a compliment.  Fists of fury is a great way to train.

im a irl boxer and this game is amazing i love the fists of fury mode its like padwork were you can change the difficulty the bags are way better than totf1 and very similar to real life i would definitely recommend this for training

Game feels as close to boxing as you can imagine.

Gym experience is great, you can even train like a boxer. 

Feels like I have turned the clock back!

Basically a great game
The game is really good mainly for the training area. The only thing is nobody plays it. If your reading this review download the game & play online.
If you're in a position like myself where you're unable to attend a real boxing gym, the $20 CAD will teach you a lot more than you'd expect. Worth every dollar.
This game has finally come out of early access and so glad to see it grow o to what it is today I have been playing since January 2024 2 yeara in and my favorite boxing game
I love playing this Game and the devs are great. They do not only great work but also great Support for the players 😜

developer_response.body:
Hi, 
This sounds like a problem with the controller.  The things I can suggest is making sure battery is up to date, restart headset, un install and re install game and make sure you have enough available space for storage. 
Let us know is this works and please consider changing your review. We have no control over hardware issues.

Thank you for the review update and thanks for playing  ! 🙏  Hope one of these helped with the issue. 🥊🥊🥊
Aaron 
Golden Gloves VR

In my opinion this is one of the best Boxing Games in vr you can Train very good and learn new moves.
What a great game honestly nothing bad to say I’m having great fun on it.
worst boxing game.. No feeling

Sorry to hear you are not happy with Golden Gloves. Can you elaborate more on what you mean by “no feeling” ?

reviews.review_description: Ik vind de fists of fury echt het leukste om te spelen ik speel altijd op 150% snelheid maar ik vind het stom dat je na 3 keer missen opnieuw moet beginnen. Ik wil graag kunnen blijven verder slaan. Is er een mogelijkheid dat dit word toegevoegd dat endless echt endless is en niet na 3 keer missen opnieuw moeten beginnen?

W game 
Good game
Boxing movement is Legit!! Love the mini games and training, and multiplayer actually works

The devs clearly know boxing, and it shows. The gym equipment physics feel nearly 1:1 with the real thing. Multiplayer can be tricky due to latency (usually Wi-Fi related), but the gym training and minigames are where this game really shines. Worth it for that alone.

Can’t wait to see what this game brings!

reviews.review_title: Feedback After Playing Golden Gloves on
reviews.review_description:
I think Golden Gloves is an amazing game — it’s such a fun and engaging way to stay active, especially when the targets rush toward you and you have to react quickly with punches. The game has a ton of potential.

That said, I do have one complaint. Over time, the highest speed setting starts to feel too slow. I believe this is because the target patterns become repetitive very quickly. The game could really benefit from more variety and challenge.

Here are a few ideas that I think could improve the experience:
	•	Introduce new mechanics or a faster training mode with increased speed.
	•	Add more diverse target patterns to keep players on their toes.
	•	Implement different punching techniques or target types.
	•	Include more feedback for mistakes — for example, a visual or audio cue when you miss a target or hit it with the wrong hand, rather than just losing a life.
	•	When the pole comes at you and you’re supposed to duck, a clearer “fail” animation or cue could help players understand they messed up, making it feel more responsive and immersive.

These changes could really enhance both the challenge and the replayability of the game. Thanks for creating such a fun and promising experience!

developer_response.body:
Hi,

Thank you so much for the detailed review—we really appreciate the time you took to share your thoughts. I’ve added your feedback to our request list. A few of your suggestions are already in the planning stages for future updates, and I’ll make sure the rest are reviewed by our team.

Quick question regarding the visual cues—for example, the duck and slip bars. We currently have audio, a red visual flash, and haptics enabled. I just wanted to check if all of those are working properly on your end.

You're also welcome to share additional feedback anytime on our Discord—I'm CoachAaronSloan on the Golden Gloves VR server.

I also noticed your initial review was 5 stars, but it was later updated to 3. If there was anything specific that led to the change, I’d really value the insight so we can continue improving.

We’ve got some exciting updates on the way, including an AI mitt coach currently in development!

Thanks again for the support!
– Aaron

reviews.review_description: The pad work in the ring is the best thing about this game although i have not done multi player i did fight the pve guys but just knocked them all out in 2 rounds but the gym is top notch,  thank you for a great workout,  47 & im now rocking a 13 stone rock solid body with speed accuracy & power all thanks to the quest headset i bought 4 years ago

reviews.review_title: Good - Can you add bHaptics support
reviews.review_description:
Hi devs. Liking the game and the way the AI throws punches and defends in single player. Am waiting on the full matchmaking multiplayer. 

Could you add bHaptics support (I have the tactvisor) so you can feel the punches in your face and arms when blocking (tactsleeve). Would appreciate this so much.

developer_response.body:
Hi,
Thank you for the review and for supporting Golden Gloves VR!
We previously integrated the bHaptics SDK but ran into some technical issues, so we decided to pause the feature for now. That said, I’ll add it back to our review list—it’s definitely something we’d like to revisit when the time allows. We’re a small team with a packed development schedule through the end of the year.

The good news: we’ve got some major updates on the way! Expect customizable avatars, a new venue, and a large-scale online tournament system. We’ll be hosting official events with USA Boxing, National Golden Gloves, and several other national federations.

If you haven’t already, join our Discord server: Golden Gloves VR. We post updates, take suggestions, and set up PvP matches there. You can find me as CoachSloan316.

Thanks again,
Aaron Sloan
Golden Gloves VR

reviews.review_description:
True facts never lie, i am someone who is training to lose weight. 
And im really hard trying to do it with VR and supplements, ive made a instagram channel, vr.fitwith.crownjax and everything is there as proof

Training mode:

Target practicing with targets rushing at you what you need to hit, constantly coming faster and faster
Focus ball: hitting the ball rapidly without missing
Dummy: speaks for itself
Practice training, sparring: against AI, sometimes feels a bit odd, because the physics are kinda different but overlooking that part and its a real banger

You fight in an open gym(area) what you can select,
Professionals do use GoldenGlovesVr for matches, from weekly to
Monthly based top prioritized matches
Compatible leaderbord constantly updated

Developer does listen to feedback and brings out updates constanly, this way the game keeps evolving and is not stuck as most Boxing games

Single Player mode just in case you have no internet
Multiplayer speaks for itself
Custome matches are preparable as well

Cons:
Physics can be better sometimes there is no response
Referee voice sound starting a match is not there

Outside of this its a definite great game, no bullsh*t straight up great, if you ignore the fact of the con selection its great and thats what i have

Conclusion, this game is WAY better then ToTF, without a doubt, if you have the chance get this game and start today your road of a boxer.!

This game teach you how important defence stance in boxing.
Punching keep bomb you if your hands down.

Oppent keep eating space, dont let you do easy-outboxing

They really good at close fight.

But font is xucked up, 

Slap fountain pen lover who charge on font.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and feedback — the font update is coming soon! 👍

We’ve got a lot more in the works, including custom avatars and a large-scale online tournament system to support our eUSA Boxing and eGolden Gloves ecosystem.

We’re just getting started! 🥊
Join our Golden Gloves VR Discord to stay in the loop and be part of the community!

reviews.review_description: This game has the potential to be a good simulator for defensive drills if it improves on the head movement mechanics in the game. I don’t think enough was put into the defense in this game outside of basic blocks…not even the computers/characters you fight have any real head movement or footwork…if this game emphasized boxing off the line and moving your head it would be a whole lot better.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for playing and for sharing your feedback! 🥊

We started with a basic single-player AI setup but quickly had to shift focus to multiplayer. That said, we absolutely plan to keep improving the single-player experience and I’ll work to prioritize some smaller updates soon.

We’ve got some exciting things in the pipeline, including custom avatars for PvP and a large-scale online tournament system to support our official UsA Boxing and Golden Gloves esports.  We’re a small team, but we’re in this for the long haul and committed to building something great! 👊

reviews.review_description: I won’t compare to other boxing games. From my perspective as someone who is trying to get in shape, this app is what I was looking for. The mechanics for slips, rolls, and head movement is impressive. I enjoy this as a tool for shadow boxing and working on defensive movement. It’s helping me with my reflexes and coordination, the opponent isn’t spamming me, appear to be throwing real combos. It’s helping my snap my punches back to cover too.  Yes I’m getting hit, but I’m practicing defense which I can’t do unless I have a coach or sparring partner. I can’t always hit my bag in the garage; so this is perfect for late night workouts. Pads are cool but I wish it would allow for missed punches without failing me.
I hope devs continue to make this game better. Supporting so more people will buy and devs add more stuff and make it more realistic boxing training, spar and fights.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for playing Golden Gloves VR!  You’re  support truly means a lot!
We’ve got a lot of exciting updates on the way, including customizable avatars and a large-scale online tournament system designed to power our eUSA Boxing and eGoldenGloves ecosystem.

We always welcome feedback and are committed to growing with the community. We’re a small team, but we’re in it for the long haul.

Join our Golden Gloves VR Discord to stay up to date, share your thoughts, and see what’s coming next.
We’re just getting started!

— Aaron Sloan
Golden Gloves VR

– Aaron Sloan, Golden Gloves VR

reviews.review_description: The gameplay is great and the boxers are more dynamic that TOTF, however the punches seem to land even though you've blocked or slipped a punch

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks so much for the review and for supporting our game — we really appreciate it!

The blocks should be working correctly, but I’d love to hear more about your experience if something felt off. As for the slips, they can definitely be tricky in single player. If we make the target too light, it becomes a bit too easy to win. That said, this is just the first phase of our single-player AI, and we’ve got plans to improve it over time.

We’re a small team and have been focused heavily on developing our multiplayer systems and tournament features. If you have questions or want a sneak peek at what’s coming, feel free to reach out on our Golden Gloves VR Discord.

Welcome to Golden Gloves! 🥊

7-20-25
Hi,

reviews.review_title: good and fun but i have a question
reviews.review_description:
this is a good boxing game and its pretty realistic in its movements....however ( and hopefully one of the devs will reply ) i cant seem to inflict as much damage as the AI opponent im boxing against ?  so i can out point them and hit them more but rthey inflict way more damage on the scorecard ? am i doing something wrong.....?  please advise ?

other than that , great game and after a weights workout at the real gym i often put this on for a 30 mnute cardio session....it really tires you out

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review! I can explain the damage difference between you and our AI pretty easily.

The health system is different between you and the AI so the damage shown on the scorecards are not measured the same. You are not doing anything wrong and this is something that we are looking to fix in the future, its just not a top priority right now.

The AI currently is also made to be tankier with stronger hits to compensate for its simplicity. As we improve the AI their damage and health will come down to be more in line with the players own stats.

Hope that explanation helps and if you have any other questions please join our discord so you can talk to the devs - https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

reviews.review_description:
This is not a game. It's a training simulator 
for boxers. The mitt work is genius, and the bag mechanics are so genuine as well as the sparring.

I’ve do local fights and spars around my city every once in a while, and after also testing other related boxing games. This has a lot going for it such as the training and career. The ai are always improving and customizable, the dev’s are really responsive and humble, and this game has given great experiences and genuinely helped me as a fighter IRL.

I love VR boxing games, so I really tried to give this game a chance, but it's terrible and I'm fairly convinced most of the reviews must be fake.

The game is a barely functional demo. There is no matchmaking system, and you must spend hours in an empty discord server to try to find people to play with. It took me 4 days of asking before I found someone to play with at a time I was available, and waited around in an empty lobby for 30 minutes until the guy who said he wanted to spar finally showed up.

If this game is really that good, why isn't anybody playing it?

The PvE is lackluster and worse than both Creed and Thrill of the Fight 1.

The PvP is basically impossible to play due to the low player count, uses half body standard Meta avatars, and has far worse physics/mechanics than Thrill of the Fight 2

The free play gym is uninspired and is basically just a bunch of different targets to hit. None of which provide any satisfying feedback or unique experience.

The only thing I enjoy in this game is the Fists of Fury pad work mini game. It works well in single player, and is well designed to give an enjoyable flow state experience. However, the combo patterns become very repetitive so it loses its appeal quickly. The hit detection/lag issues in the multiplayer version of the mini game also makes it unenjoyable.

I've patiently waited a few months after spending my $25 in hopes that I would start seeing some development, but updates are almost nonexistent in that timeline.

The discrepancy between the marketing for this game and the real life product is on the threshold of being a scam.

developer_response.body:
Hi Jon,

I’m sorry to hear that you’re feeling this way. We truly appreciate your feedback and want to address your concerns. Since launch, we’ve been actively pushing updates and continuously improving the game based on player input.

Regarding matchmaking, because we are in early access, we encourage players to coordinate matches via Discord while we grow our player base. If we were to enable quick play across multiple servers at this stage, there’s a chance you might end up in an empty lobby, which we want to avoid.

As for the gym, could you clarify what you mean? The gym in Golden Gloves VR is designed to replicate a real-world boxing gym, complete with training equipment and realistic physics. We’re always open to suggestions on how to improve it further!

Regarding avatars, we initially integrated Meta’s avatars to get PvP functionality up and running quickly, allowing players to experience multiplayer boxing sooner rather than later. However, we’ve been hard at work developing a custom avatar system, which is a major project and has taken more time to complete. This is also tied to our efforts in expanding eGoldenGloves and building a robust esports framework.

The reason this update cycle has been longer than usual is because we’re working on significant advancements—including the new gym update, which is about to be released, and the custom avatars, which will roll out in the next few weeks. As always, we’ll continue refining and improving these features after release, which is exactly why we remain in early access.

I also want to clarify that our Discord is very much active, with nearly 2,000 members—a strong community for an early access game that is purposely growing at a steady pace. Our team is highly responsive, always open to feedback, and happy to answer questions. In fact, we recently introduced open Zoom calls that I personally host to discuss the game’s roadmap and connect directly with the community.

We have no intention of scamming our players—we are fully committed to Golden Gloves VR for the long haul. We appreciate your support and would love to hear more of your thoughts on how we can make the experience even better.

Thanks again for your feedback!

Best,
Aaron Sloan 
Golden Gloves VR

reviews.review_title: Fist of Fury : Left mode not working
reviews.review_description: You mentioned that you’ve already identified the bugs and plan to release a fixed version, but as of this morning, I’m still experiencing the same issues. The bugs only occur when I enable the left-mode setting, which I use because I’m left-handed. Could you please address this problem? By the way, it’s a great game overall

developer_response.body:
Hi,
I have 2 others that reported this bug as well. We have been unable to consistency replicate in house. However, we will push a blanket update tomorrow that will hopefully correct the issue. Also if you will reach out to us on our Golden Gloves VR Discord we can get additional information to help track down the issue. You can reach me direct there at CoachSloan316. Sincerely appreciate you bringing this to our attention. We will get it resolved! 🥊
Aaron Sloan 
Golden Gloves VR

Update: We found the bug. We will be pushing up a major update tomorrow with a fix for the mini game. Thanks for the feedback and letting us know!

Update: were you able to download the new fix for the left hand mists mode ?

reviews.review_description: I've had this game for quite a while.  At first, I didn't like it much, but now it is one of my favorites.  The AI opponents have gotten much better, but need more variety of fighting styles.  I would happily pay for additional AI opponents. Really like being able to adjust things in custom matches, but wish custom matches were available in MR.  I don't play PvP, so I can't say anything about that.  Training part is very good.  Definitely worth buying.

I’ve played creed both totf and other vr boxing games this game is closest to what real boxing is the gym  I can hit the bags and hear the snap on my hits or get a rhythm with the double end or speed bag and hit the mitts and sweat all this while I can keep a a boxing technique so while the other games may have more players this game feels more like the real thing itsgreat if you are a boxer or train and the career is decent there could be more added for the ai like different boxing styles  and  head movement footwork I hope more is added in the future

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback and review! We definitely plan to keep adding more to the game and improve our AI over time.

reviews.review_description: Solid game lots of different stuff to do

reviews.review_title: Ring size doesn’t fit your guardian
reviews.review_description:
I was really excited about this game as a big thrill of the fight fan.

Overall, it seems like a really solid alternative, but I found it to be completely unplayable given that the ring size game does not match your place space which makes it incredibly hard to know where you can move to.￼

This feature is implemented I will be excited to give it another try.

developer_response.body:
Hi,
Can you explain more on exactly what you mean ? There is not really a way to make the size of the ring fit a players guardian. All guardians are made at different sizes.  There is a joystick movement option in the menu setting to allow you to move around the ring and a toggle to show your play area inside the game too. Feel free to reach out to me on our Discord and I will give you a personal tour of the game, answer questions and go over what’s in the works. 🥊
I am coachsloan316 in discord. 
https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

Hi, 
Wanted to follow up and see if you could provide more detail ? 
Thanks 
Aaron

reviews.review_description: Actually the best boxing on here

I played thrill of fighting 2 early access, then played golden gloves what a difference so much better physics and playability.
Very good quality game, so yous can remove the Unity splash screen
with pro subscription or use Unity 6, usually Unity splash screen is associated with lower quality apps/games.
World multi player would be great, right now I only see the possibility of playing via invite code, which I have not played but has to be better than thrill of fighting 2 surely.
A lot of VR games tend to have awkward glitches and stuff, granted I have just returned to VR after about a year but this is smoothest I have played of any.
Fantastic job by the coder(s), keep up the work and updates, be worth it
Better than TOTF2

reviews.review_title: Thrill of the fight 2 is absolutely rubb
reviews.review_description: Thrill of the fight 2 is absolutely rubbish, it needs a lot of work. As professional boxer, golden gloves  is not bad needs a bit more work on it, but it's much better then thrill  fight 2 , once you get more boxes, on and multiplier and improve on the AI everything what you're trying to do it will be  top boxing game number 1 .

I don’t know what happened but the game is broken for me it used to work but now it starts me in the crowd in front of the weigh-in. There is no possible way to get up on the stage and therefore no way to start the game. I would like to get back to hitting the mits. I tried a reinstall but get the same issue.  Please fix with an update.

developer_response.body:
We just found the issue. Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We are making changes now and will have a new build up with fixes in next couple of hours.

FollowUp: Were you able to update and get into the game now that we fixed the bug you reported

Hi, 
Were you able to play the game again with the new updates ?

reviews.review_description: Flow and movement in PVP is great. Has a good system to help prevent spam punches as well. I’m not a big fan of the Meta avatars but it sounds like a new avatar system is coming soon.

The bouts seem to be in development.  Hear me out.  The training is above the bar compared to ANY game out there (I have ALL boxing games).

They have something similar to mitt work.  They have a gym but taking the gym and putting it in mixed reality is the BEST.  You’re able to have your own personal gym with great punching response.

If you buy this game…buy it for the training.  The actual bouts will improve.

Only way to find a match is to join the discord and pray someone is down to play no way you’ll find a match by just hitting search. Gameplay is awful only head and arms no body no legs calibration is wack and getting knockdowns is impossible. TOTF2 came out today only 10 bucks and great sad I wasted money on this garbage.

developer_response.body:
Sorry you didn't have a good experience with the game. We currently have a low player count for multiplayer but a new matchmaking system and better player bodies are coming in the near future.

https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

reviews.review_title: Not buying for the gameplay but...
reviews.review_description: what I did buy this for was the training mode and I love it, especially the mitts. I love the mitts, I do wish there were a few more angles for customization of my punches but this is by far the best vr boxing training game

reviews.review_title: How do i go in Jake Pauls Gym UPDATE gym
reviews.review_description: Pls make jake paul update today also i can't talk in the discord discord user is Covr679

developer_response.body:
Hi,
Thank you so much for the support ! Sorry we haven’t made it live in the game yet. Coming very soon and we will make an announcement. We have some very cool things planned around the live release. 
If you join our Discord and reach out to me I will send you some GGVR merch.
My username there is @CoachSloan316. 
🥊🥊🥊   https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

reviews.review_description:
I am blown away by this game! I am so blown away by the developer’s ability to make this as immersive and real as they did. The double end bags move just like the real thing! I love to get on and go against the CPUs while I wait for a live match. Each CPU gets gradually more difficult, making the desire to progress to the top that much sweeter. If it’s too easy or difficult, there’s a function that allows you to adjust the settings (opponent’s punches are faster, cause more damage, etc.) so the fun and challenge continues!
The multiplayer function is so cool! Pitting your skills up against REAL opponents is the best feature of the game! I love interacting with the community of gamers who are also enjoying this game. The game still has a few bugs. They’re not terrible, but you will sometimes spawn outside the ring before a match starts, but the majority of the time, you will spawn back in the ring before the bell rings. One time, the gloves will sometimes come off the avatar and float around the ring, but that hasn’t happened again in months.

Seriously, check this game out TODAY!!
Lots of features and constant updates !

The game is very good, not as precise and configurable as TOTF and the AIs don't move enough. It would be nice if they moved like Ultra Boxing VR. The multiplayer mode has only one flaw and not the least. Joystick movement should not be possible. With a real environment of 2m x 2m, we can very well only move physically. This is more realistic than going back and forth with the joystick. I spend my time having to use it. Let the opponents stay close like when playing against the AI. As it stands, the game is a little expensive, but I'm having fun

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the feedback!

More configurations and improvements are coming in the future and our AI is still being developed and improved as well!

For multiplayer we chose to make Joystick movement the default because most of our players dont have large enough play areas to move freely so this still allows them to play the game. In the future we plan to have the option to disable joystick movement and host sessions where you aren't allowed to use the joystick for those players who want to fight others who also only move their body.

reviews.review_description:
The AI is not ready for public consumption. Super easy to avoid and knock down, can't handle circling. This leaves multiplayer (most important part) and minigames.

Minigames are ok, not amazing.

Multiplayer looks like it might be awesome but it is really hard to connect to people even through the discord community. The community seems very nice, and I am looking forward to try this, though I suspect the people there are super good at the game and would be bored with a totf junky like me.

I think the multiplayer community would build a lot faster if we could specify a named room code. The server would only need to have a hash with the existing number system when creating a new server to implement this. A second big thing would be to let me put an ai in the ring with me while I wait. If this was possible, then "clubs" would start forming and I could just join the same room to play, as people came and went.

Anyway, after playing for a few hours this game feels early access. Should be an early access price.
Really easy to pick up and a great work out

reviews.review_title: Loved it , confused between 4 or 5 stars
reviews.review_description:
Must play for boxing lovers.
The best part is we can play it in tidy place too,also Very good mini games, vfx works good,Thanks for career mode.
except character model everything is good for VR games.

reviews.review_title: The mini games are done really well
reviews.review_description: I'll start by saying that this game is worth buying just for the training mode. It is done really well, as well as stimulating reflexes and coordination, it also greatly improves resistance. It would be nice to include side hook saves in Fast and Fury mode. It would be more complete and very training.
Why I go to multiple player nobody joins this game is rip off don’t get this get creed instead this junk

developer_response.body:
Our game is in early access and multiplayer is fairly new. Our player count is low right now that is why we link you to our Discord server in game. Joining the Discord server will allow you to easily find matches in multiplayer.

You can join the server here - https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

reviews.review_description:
I couldn’t find players with quick match. If you don’t have mp Thrill of the fight is much better. 
You need to make price like 2-4$ or even free. When you have enough players you can make it 30$. But for now I don’t see a reason to buy this game.

developer_response.body: Hi, I told you in the Discord that we are currently in an Open Beta for our multiplayer and that is the reason for our low player count. We have a thriving community on Discord with players willing to hop on at almost anytime. All you have to do is post a message asking for a fight.

reviews.review_title: Hi. Not a good one as advertised.
reviews.review_description: Nothing even close to the boxing fight. Just for small kids to move their bodies.

developer_response.body:
Can you provide more feedback ? I have boxed and coached for 15 years  and would disagree that this is not close to a boxing match. Also this is being used at USA Boxing camps and will be their official esport. We have very very few young players in our servers right now as well. 
Thanks 
Aaron Sloan
Owner

reviews.review_description: Tried it again after waiting for updates and the game is not quite there.  For some reason it's just not that satisfying.  I have other boxing games that are tech demos but more fun to play.
When I first started playing this game is really fun like you can practice boxing

As an avid Thrill of the Fight player doing 12 rounds of outclassed I did not expect too much from this game going into it. To my surprise it was actually a great new experience within the boxing genre, a breath of fresh air from replaying Creed and TOTF.

It has its own unique style of fighting which can certainly work up a sweat. I really love that liver/body shots have an impact and cause reaction from the AI.
Like others have mentioned, there is still some work required on the AI but from reading the comments the developers seem to be working actively on this. 

The only major thing that impacted my game play was trying to control the fight within the Oculus Guardian, the inability to move or push back the AI by walking into them can cause you to be in awkward areas in real life. Not sure if this is just an issue I was having on Quest 2 being unable to move the characters with joysticks when the fight starts.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the great feedback! I'm glad to hear that you enjoyed the game so much. The current AI should back up if you get too close to him but only if he is not currently attacking. If he is in the middle of attacking you he should finish his combo and then move into his normal fighting range. We also offer a joystick movement option in the pause room called "Orbital Movement". This allows you to move with the joysticks around the ring but you are orbiting your opponent. This means that if you press forward on the joystick you will move towards the opponent regardless of what direction you are facing, same for pressing left and right. You will move in those directions relative to your opponent.

We do a bad job of explaining the controls currently, but there is a control map image in the pause room and you can get there by pressing the Start button (has 3 horizontal lines) on the left controller. Once in the pause room you can select and drag the left column with the buttons to reach the "Control" button on the bottom and it will show the control map.

We are always looking for more people to talk to on our Discord and we give frequent updates there. Hope to see you there soon - https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

reviews.review_description:
Im not sure how anyone could give this game more than 3 stars at this price point, it’s priced like a released game, has too many bugs right now.
The Game itself is good but games that are out now are cheaper and better

Pros: you can walk around the gym,
Career mode (kind of),
Some training equipment not seen on other games,
Clocks with interval timers isn’t something I’ve seen and it’s a big plus 

Cons: Opponent going outside your playing area,
Opponent freezing, making noises sometimes, never dropping their gloves (it’s nice to be challenged a bit but it seems excessive), being able to punch like 4 feet away???, when you get close it just makes a bunch of punching noises like the guy is pounding you but your in a blocking stance, it seems like your opponent punches so quickly you don’t see them swing (this might be what they’re going for it just doesn’t come off as natural),
height difference is unfortunate to say the least, I’m pretty short and even with the height calibration my opponent is still a lot taller than me,
Glove getting stuck in between the opponents constant block
Opponent’s are very repetitive, doesn’t seem to have their own style like other games just the same things over and over 

Overall I can see the potential in the game and I can’t wait for the updates as I hear the development team is working hard. I will definitely revisit this review on further updates.

Suggestions to add: SLOW the game down a bit. This game makes it seem like you constantly have to be swinging when boxing is usually based on looking for your opponents openings (not much given here).
being able to play your own music in the gym (even just launching YouTube),
Mini interactive games possibly with a “coach”
Making career mode like NHL games not just saying next,
Having time in the gym contribute to your players ranking,
Let us in the gym rings to shadow box or give option to train with the guy in the ring at the gym.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the honest review! A lot of the things you talked about are planned to be added in the future, but there are a few ideas you mentioned that I am interested in discussing further. We have a Discord server setup for the game were you can talk with the devs directly, and I would appreciate it if you could message me there.

I am Wild on the server - https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

Hi, 
Have you got to try our game again recently ? 
We would love to get your feedback.

reviews.review_title: Worst amongst all boxing games out there
reviews.review_description: This is almost a crime to release such game.

developer_response.body:
Hi cushold, 
Can you give us any feed back that constructive? I have been coaching or fighting for over 20 years and i can assure you the mechanics and closeness to “real” boxing is better than any game now on the market. We also demoed to dozens of boxers in my gym and over 150 national level boxers at Golden Gloves Nationals in Tulsa and the feedback was the same. 
That being said we do have a lot to work on and that’s why we are on the early release to work out bugs. I would rather have you tell us what you would like to see different and we will do our best. We are committed to this long term. 
If you would follow us on our discord to provide feedback and see what we are working on daily. 
https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm
Thanks 
Aaron Sloan 
Engine Room VR

reviews.review_description:
I'm giving this 4 stats because of what I can definitely see as potential but realistically it is a 3 star game at the moment. I'm an avid thrill of the fight player

First the pros: the gym itself is really cool. I love the physics of all the training tools like the heavy bag, speed bag, double end bag etc. They all have tutorials and already feel at least as good of not better than the thrill of the fight equivalents. Particularly the heavy bag really gives the feeling of weight that is hard to describe but really cool.

Now the cons: there really is just one but it's a big one, the actual fighting is absolutely terrible right now. The fighters are robotic and just not at all realistic, even compared to thrill of the fight where many people have similar complaints. Playing the two back to back is like night and day from an engagement perspective.

That being said the developer seems like a really nice guy who is actively taking user feedback and working on an entirely new ai system for the fighters so hopefully that will lead to some major improvements in the near future

developer_response.body:
Hi Conorlevy,

Thank you for the honest review, its greatly appreciated!

We put a lot of work into the gym scene trying to make the training equipment as realistic as possible. We plan to add more equipment and hopefully some workout routines in the future as well.

I do agree that our current AI isn't up to our standards for quality yet and I have some good news. We are currently working on a new version of the AI that addresses all of the bugs and pain points we had with the first version and will hopefully have a few additional bells and whistles. Its not out yet and there's no set release date for it yet but we will make sure it is well known when it releases. Internally we have it fighting in bouts but it needs a lot of training and there are some weird bugs that need to be ironed out. We also plan to record more animations and clean up all the existing ones so they look better.

Once again thank you for the honest review and I hope to see you again when the new AI drops!

reviews.review_description: Cant wait to see the finished product. The fact that you can use this as a training tool makes it more than fun.  Great cardio and its functional... Great game and idea, hats off to you guys. Really have a good thing here...My son loves it, and it's keeping him in shape when we can't make it to the gym.

developer_response.body:
Hi, 
Thank you for the review ! We will keep working hard to make it better ! 
I noticed you changed your review from 5 stars to 3 stars. If you let us know what might have changed your mind we can address it. 
Also follow us on discord to give  feedback. 
https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm
Aaron Sloan 
Engine Room VR

reviews.review_title: great game for how early access it is
reviews.review_description: i just got the game and from the 15 minutes i’ve played i’d say it’s amazing for how good it is already, the fights are realistic unlike creed your stamina isn’t determined by in game characters stamina it’s determined by yours. the arenas and just all around detail you don’t see in other boxing games is amazing.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review Austin!

We are glad to hear you are enjoying the game! When we first set out to make this game we wanted it to be as realistic as possible, that includes good graphics and forcing the users to get in shape if they want to be able to keep up with our more professional boxer AIs. We have more venues and boxers planned as well as training material we want to add to the game to teach players how to box properly.

We would appreciate it if you could join our Discord. We post updates there and the devs interact with the community and respond a lot faster there!

https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

reviews.review_description: Very good

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking time to review. Follow us on discord if you would like to see what’s in the works or to expand on your feedback!
Thanks 
Aaron Sloan 
Engine Room VR
https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

UPDATE:  11/24
HI 
We pushed an update yesterday that corrected a lot of the issues with punch mechanics, upgraded the graphics and performance as well. More to come.

reviews.review_description: Golden Glove VR is an amazing game tho it’s has a few issues. The AI needs a lot of improvement, you get hit from random shots that should not have landed and it is just throwing the same combos in a loop which makes the AI easy to read. It would be good for the AI to have some awareness. When you put pressure on them they don’t back up and they barley defend themselves, and they just keep walking into you. Other than that the rest of the game is good, the gym mechanics they have built into the game feel realistic. More realistic than totf and eoc. This game will be more when enjoyable when multiplayer is out, and when they have made the AI more realistic it will 100% give totf a run for it’s money. Also developers if your reading this when will multiplayer release.

developer_response.body:
HI, 
Thanks for your review !! We are glad that you like the game so far. We are currently reworking most of the AI from the ground up and see some promising improvements in house. We will be adding more animations and physics features as well that will make opponent not so robotic. As for multi player we have tested a crude demo and plan on focusing hard on this in the coming few weeks. We have a lot planned that we will be announcing soon and would like for you to join our discord if you haven’t already to keep up with you updates and to give more feedback. 
Thanks again for taking time to review. This is what we need !!!
https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

reviews.review_description: I own a gym & we train boxers. My daughter is currently a ranked fighter by USA Amateur Boxing. We use vr training in many different ways. Met the developers of this app at boxing nationals. Great app. Currently great & still in development. Download & be ready for impressive updates to come. Highly recommended. Thanks for an excellent product.

developer_response.body:
Hi, 
Thanks for taking time to try our game at nationals !!! We will keep working hard to improve and keep regular updates coming. Follow us on discord to see what’s inn the works and to keep the feedback coming !
https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm

reviews.review_description:
This game is fun. Not better than TOTF which is in a league by itself for now. But this game is worth the money and it is very fun.

It has the potential to be better than TOTF because of the constant updates which is non existent in TOTF.

This game has a career mode with ranking systems. The mechanics on this game are great. Needs more work but in due time, It will be hard to compete with this game.

Great boxing VR game and it really gives you a fantastic workout.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review ! We have a lot still in the works for this title and will continue to do our best to improve the game. We pushed up some updates to punch mechanics, Graphics and performance last night.  Follow us on discord to keep giving us input and to see what we are currently working on. It’s much appreciated!
https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm
Thanks 
Aaron Sloan 
Engine Room VR

reviews.review_description: Finally a worthy competitor for ToTF emerges (although ToTF2 will likely be very good). The graphics are great and the game features lots of unique ideas that are missing from similar VR boxing games. The fighting mechanics still have a lot of room for improvement but in some places the movement around the ring feels even more realistic than ToTF. The sound effects seem really broken at the moment, as well as the impact effects and haptic feedback. I will be checking back frequently to see how this game turns out, keep up the great work guys and I will definitely update this to a 5* review!

developer_response.body:
Hi Sam !
Thanks for the feedback ! This is what we are looking for.
We have been working on the punch mechanics and we believe the update we pushed up yesterday has addressed the main issues. Give it a try and let us know what you think. One of the main problem was the hooks not landing. I made the game  sounds pretty “Arcady” recently because players were having trouble when we had “real” punch sounds on each player. It was hard to distinguish who was landing what. We will look to add better audio a little later. We also have limited sound bites and they loop sometimes, but this will be updated soon and is currently being worked on in house. We also updated the graphics on yesterdays push among a few other performance upgrades. Let us know what you think on discord and keep the input coming.  We will keep working hard to make this the best boxing game on the market. 
https://discord.gg/t8NsuFfWSm
Thanks Again 
Aaron Sloan 
Engine Room VR
